questions.question: What does biodiversity refer to?
questions.explanation: Biodiversity means the variety of plants, animals, and microorganisms living in an ecosystem.

questions.question: Which of the following is a major source of biodiversity?
questions.explanation: Forests provide habitats for many species and support biodiversity.

questions.question: Which act was introduced to protect wildlife in India?
questions.explanation: The Wildlife Protection Act was implemented in 1972 to protect wildlife.

questions.question: Which project was launched to protect tigers in India?
questions.explanation: Project Tiger was launched in 1973 to conserve the tiger population.

questions.question: What is the main aim of wildlife conservation?
questions.explanation: Wildlife conservation aims to protect biodiversity and maintain ecological balance.
questions.source_section: Conservation of Forest and Wildlife in India

questions.question: Which type of forest is considered most valuable for conservation?
questions.explanation: Reserved forests receive the highest level of protection.
questions.source_section: Types and Distribution of Forest and Wildlife Resources

questions.question: Protected forests are mainly protected from which of the following?
questions.explanation: Protected forests are safeguarded from further depletion.
questions.source_section: Types and Distribution of Forest and Wildlife Resources

questions.question: Which movement involved people hugging trees to stop them from being cut?
questions.explanation: The Chipko Movement involved villagers hugging trees to prevent deforestation.

questions.question: What are sacred groves?

options.B: Protected forest areas due to religious beliefs

questions.explanation: Sacred groves are forest patches protected because of cultural or religious beliefs.

questions.question: Which animal is mainly protected under Project Tiger?
questions.explanation: Project Tiger focuses on conserving the tiger population.

questions.question: Which of the following is an example of a wildlife sanctuary?
questions.explanation: Kaziranga National Park is famous for wildlife conservation.

questions.question: Which of the following is a threat to biodiversity?
questions.explanation: Deforestation destroys habitats and reduces biodiversity.
questions.source_section: Conservation of Forest and Wildlife in India

questions.question: Which state first started Joint Forest Management in India?
questions.explanation: Joint Forest Management started in Odisha in 1988.

questions.question: Which animal is protected in Kaziranga National Park?
questions.explanation: Kaziranga National Park is famous for the one-horned rhinoceros.

questions.question: Which of the following helps conserve forests?
questions.explanation: Afforestation means planting more trees and protecting forests.
questions.source_section: Conservation of Forest and Wildlife in India

questions.question: Which species was added to the protected list in 1991?
questions.explanation: Plants were included in the protected species list in 1991.

questions.question: Which forest category covers more than half of India's forest land?
questions.explanation: Reserved forests account for more than half of the forest land.
questions.source_section: Types and Distribution of Forest and Wildlife Resources

questions.question: Which movement promoted community afforestation in the Himalayas?
questions.explanation: The Chipko Movement encouraged forest protection and afforestation.

questions.question: Which animal is protected in Gir National Park?
questions.explanation: Gir National Park is the only habitat of the Asiatic Lion.

questions.question: Which term refers to protecting forests for future generations?
questions.explanation: Conservation means protecting natural resources.
questions.source_section: Conservation of Forest and Wildlife in India

questions.question: Which animal is known as the king of the forest?
questions.explanation: The tiger is commonly known as the king of the forest in India.

questions.question: Which forest type may belong to communities or private individuals?
questions.explanation: Unclassed forests may belong to communities or private individuals.
questions.source_section: Types and Distribution of Forest and Wildlife Resources

questions.question: Which movement helped stop deforestation in several Himalayan regions?
questions.explanation: The Chipko Movement helped prevent tree cutting in the Himalayas.

questions.question: Which tree is commonly considered sacred in India?
questions.explanation: Peepal tree is considered sacred in many parts of India.

questions.question: What is the main goal of forest conservation?
questions.explanation: Forest conservation helps protect biodiversity and ecological balance.
